title: Modern: Last Element with .at()
description: Given an array, return the very last element using the modern `.at()` method. Use a negative index.
patternExplanation: .at(index) allows for negative indexing to easily grab elements from the end of an array without using .length.

title: Modern: Immutable Sort
description: Given an array of numbers, return a NEW sorted array in ascending order using `.toSorted()`. The original array must remain unchanged.
whatShouldHappen: Provide the same comparison logic `(a, b) => a - b`.
patternExplanation: .toSorted() is the non-mutating version of .sort(). It returns a new array, keeping the original intact.

title: Modern: Immutable Reverse
description: Given an array, return a NEW array with the elements in reverse order using `.toReversed()`. Do not modify the original array.
patternExplanation: .toReversed() returns a reversed copy of the array without mutating the original reference.

title: Modern: Replace with .with()
description: Given an array, an index, and a new value, return a NEW array where the element at that index is replaced by the new value. Use the modern `.with()` method.
patternExplanation: .with(index, value) is a convenient way to get a copy of an array with one element changed, which is a very common pattern in React state updates.

title: Modern: Find Last Match
description: Given an array of numbers, return the LAST negative number in the array using `.findLast()`. If none exist, return `undefined`.
whatShouldHappen: The callback should check if the number is less than 0.
patternExplanation: .findLast() starts searching from the end of the array towards the beginning, returning the first value that matches the condition.

title: Modern: Immutable Splice
description: Given an array, a start index, and a delete count, return a NEW array with those elements removed using `.toSpliced()`. Do not mutate the original array.
patternExplanation: .toSpliced() provides the powerful insertion/deletion capabilities of .splice() but returns a new array instead of mutating the source.

title: Modern: Depth Flatten
description: Given a nested array, flatten it to a depth of 2 using the modern `.flat()` method.
patternExplanation: .flat(depth) is the modern way to flatten nested arrays. The depth defaults to 1 if not provided.
